query: Use SDD to plan support for a `harness-security-review` recipe. Stay in the Intent and Spec phases only — do not implement.
expected_skill_contribution: The SDD recipe should explicitly name the Intent and Spec phases, clarify non-goals, produce acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then form, and resist jumping to implementation.
fixture_intent: The recipes directory and AGENTS.md as context for the new recipe; the agent must work through Intent and Spec phases only, stopping before any implementation or bead creation for implementation work.
baseline_gaps: jumps to implementation — proposes YAML recipe content or creates implementation files
skips non-goals — writes only goals without clarifying what is out of scope
produces vague requirements without acceptance criteria in testable form
expected_behavior: explicitly names the SDD phase (Intent, then Spec) before each section
clarifies non-goals explicitly before writing any spec
produces acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then or equivalent testable form
does NOT propose implementation steps or create implementation beads

query: Use SDD to drive a TDD slice for idempotence checks in the install scripts. Write the failing test spec first, then the implementation plan.
expected_skill_contribution: The SDD recipe should drive the TDD phase: RED spec first (failing test description), then minimal GREEN implementation plan, then REFACTOR step — in that named order.
fixture_intent: The install scripts as the unit under test; the agent must write a failing test specification before any implementation detail, following explicit RED→GREEN→REFACTOR discipline without editing files.
baseline_gaps: writes implementation without a failing test first — GREEN before RED
skips RED/GREEN/REFACTOR naming — uses TDD informally without explicit phase markers
edits files instead of producing a plan
expected_behavior: names the TDD phase and uses RED/GREEN/REFACTOR terminology explicitly
specifies the failing test (RED) before any implementation step
defines the minimal implementation (GREEN) as a separate step after RED
does not edit files — produces plan only

query: Run a full SDD release-readiness evaluation for the harness after the agent roster changes. Produce an explicit readiness matrix.
expected_skill_contribution: The SDD recipe should produce a release-readiness matrix covering all SDD gates (tests/docs/install/rollback/observability/blockers) with explicit pass/fail/blocked/unknown per dimension.
fixture_intent: The release-readiness doc, recipes, agent files, and scripts as the surfaces to evaluate; the agent must produce a structured matrix assessment of all required gates rather than a prose narrative.
baseline_gaps: produces prose readiness summary without per-dimension status
omits rollback or observability as dimensions — treats them as optional
uses discretionary gates — treats quality gates as optional polish rather than product requirements
expected_behavior: produces a matrix with at minimum: tests, docs, install-script safety, recipe validation, rollback, observability, open blockers
marks each dimension pass/fail/blocked/unknown — not prose
treats quality gates as product requirements not optional polish
states explicit done criteria and remaining blockers
